sectional_curvature.verdict: FLAT — triangles perfectly obey Euclidean law of cosines

ollivier_ricci.method: Approximate ORC on k=10 NN graph (1903 edges)

interpretation.why_different: Sectional curvature measures the INTRINSIC geometry of the embedding (flat because we project linearly into R^5). Ollivier-Ricci measures the EXTRINSIC geometry of the data distribution within the space (positive because objects cluster into tight neighborhoods). The space is flat. The data distribution within it is clustered/spherical.
interpretation.angular_correction: 0.000 degrees — curvature does NOT affect our axis tilt measurements. The 7.8 degree tilt is a true Euclidean rotation, not a geodesic deviation.
interpretation.for_gemini: The space itself is Euclidean (flat sectional curvature). But the data occupies a positively-curved submanifold within it (ORC=0.71). If we switched to a hyperbolic embedding, the natural geometry of the data might be better captured, but our current angle measurements are valid in flat space.
